title: 9020: death
content: Death in its various aspects is seen by Scripture as the opposite of God-given life.

title: 9021: death, natural
content:
The end of life in the present world.

Death is natural to humanity as part of the created world

ECC 12:7 See also PSA 39:5, PSA 104:29; ECC 3:18–3:21; 2PE 1:13–1:14

Death is a result of Adam's sin

ROM 5:12 There is a tension between this text and those which suggest that death is a natural aspect of being human. Some suggest that the death referred to here is spiritual rather than physical. See also GEN 2:17, GEN 3:19; ROM 6:23; 1CO 15:21–15:22

Death is universal

None can escape death

PSA 89:48 See also GEN 6:3; JOS 23:14; 2SA 14:14; JOB 30:23

Death comes to all kinds of people

JOB 21:23–21:26 See also 2SA 12:15–12:18; 2KI 4:20; PSA 37:35–37:36, PSA 49:10; ECC 3:19; LUK 16:22

Only two people did not die

GEN 5:24; 2KI 2:11 Enoch and Elijah died in the sense that their bodies were transformed from being suited to earthly life to life in heaven. They anticipated the death of Jesus Christ, whose body was not corrupted but transformed, and the transition to the new world of those who are alive when Jesus Christ comes again.

Death is irreversible

HEB 9:27 The idea of reincarnation, that after death people repeatedly return to this life in a lower or higher form, is incompatible with death being once for all and an entrance into heaven or hell after the judgment. See also JOB 16:22; PSA 115:17

Death is an aspect of human frailty

PSA 146:3–146:4 See also ISA 51:12; 1PE 1:24; ISA 40:6–40:8

Death occurs when God determines it

JOB 14:5 See also DEU 32:39; JOB 38:17; LUK 12:20; REV 1:18

The means of death

Through old age

GEN 25:8, GEN 35:29; ; JDG 8:32; ; 1KI 2:10

Through disease and accident

2KI 1:2, 2KI 1:16–1:17, 2KI 20:1 parallel 2CH 32:24; ; MRK 5:23; ; ACT 20:9

Through violence

JDG 9:5, JDG 9:52–9:54; ; 2SA 13:28–13:29; ; 1KI 15:28; ; 2KI 8:14–8:15; ; MAT 2:16, MAT 14:8–14:11

As a judgment by God

LEV 10:1–10:2; ; NUM 16:31–16:32, NUM 20:23–20:24; ; 1SA 25:38; ; 2SA 6:7, 2SA 24:15; ; 2CH 13:20; ; ACT 5:5, ACT 5:10, ACT 12:21–12:23; ; 1CO 11:29–11:30

Through a legal penalty

EXO 21:12–21:17; ; LUK 23:32, LUK 23:40–23:41

The prospect of death affects human attitudes and values

Life is short

JAS 4:14 See also JOB 7:6, JOB 8:9, JOB 9:25; PSA 39:5, PSA 89:47, PSA 90:10, PSA 102:11; ECC 6:12; ISA 38:12, ISA 40:6–40:8

People fear death

PSA 18:4–18:6, PSA 116:3; ; HEB 2:15

People should prepare for death

2KI 20:1 parallel ISA 38:1 See also PSA 39:4, PSA 90:12; AMO 4:12

The expectation of death is an incentive to serve God

ECC 9:10 See also ISA 38:18–38:19; JHN 9:4

The expectation of death is an incentive to hold material blessings lightly

1TI 6:7 See also JOB 1:21; LUK 12:13–12:21; 1CO 7:29–7:31

Death is sometimes desired to escape problems

NUM 11:11–11:15; ; 1KI 19:4; ; JOB 3:11, JOB 7:15; ; JER 8:3; ; JON 4:3; ; ACT 16:27; ; REV 9:6

Death was defeated by Jesus Christ

2TI 1:10 See also ISA 25:8; HOS 13:14; 1CO 15:24–15:26; HEB 2:14; REV 20:14

There is no death in heaven

REV 21:4 See also LUK 20:35–20:36; 2CO 5:1–5:2

title: 9022: death, of believers
content:
Believers in Jesus Christ will depart from this present life to the joy of God's presence.

Death is a normal experience for believers

ROM 14:8 See also HEB 9:27

Death can be a blessing

Because of the prospect of heaven

PHP 1:21–1:23 See also NUM 23:10; PSA 49:15, PSA 116:15; LUK 2:29; 2TI 4:6–4:8; REV 7:13–7:17, REV 14:13

Because it brings relief from trouble and problems

1KI 19:4; ; JOB 3:20, JOB 7:15; ; 2TH 1:7

At death believers are with Christ

They are in his presence

LUK 23:43 See also 2CO 5:8; 1TH 5:10

They are safe and secure

REV 2:11 The second death is the final banishment of unbelievers from God's presence. See also ROM 14:8; REV 20:6

They have the prospect of a new order of things

REV 21:4 See also 2TI 4:6–4:8; REV 22:3–22:5

The defeat of death

Death is vanquished for believers

1CO 15:55 See also ROM 8:36; 2TI 1:10; HEB 2:14; REV 20:14

Death is to be reversed in resurrection

Though some of the above passages suggest that eternal life in Jesus Christ's presence is experienced immediately at death, others such as the following link these blessings with resurrection at the final coming of Jesus Christ: JHN 11:23; ; 1CO 15:51–15:54; ; 1TH 4:16

The death of believers is likened to sleep

Implying rest, renewing and awaking

ACT 7:59–7:60 Sleep does not imply lack of consciousness but rather rest, renewing and awaking. In the NT it is only used of believers. See also DEU 31:16; DAN 12:2; MRK 5:39; JHN 11:11–11:14; ACT 13:36; 1CO 15:6, 1CO 15:17–15:18; 1TH 4:13–4:16

Implying peace and comfort

LUK 16:22 Abraham's side suggests a welcome to a place of peace and protection. See also GEN 49:33; 2KI 22:20; ISA 57:1–57:2; LUK 16:25

Death and the recognition of others

1TH 4:17 “caught up together” implies recognition. See also GEN 25:8 “gathered to his people” (literally “to his father's kin”) is a common OT expression and implies continued existence together; JOB 3:13–3:14; MAT 17:4; MRK 9:4; LUK 16:24; 1CO 13:12

Believers can face death without fear

They die in relationship with God

HEB 2:14–2:15 See also PSA 23:4; PRO 14:32

They die trusting the Lord

HEB 11:13, HEB 11:21–11:22

They receive comfort in facing death

JHN 11:25–11:26 See also ISA 25:8; 1CO 15:26; REV 1:18

In death they can glorify God

PHP 1:20

In this believers are to be envied

NUM 23:10

Believers often hated the idea of death

See also JOB 17:13–17:16; PSA 9:13; ISA 38:10; ROM 8:36; 1CO 15:55–15:56; 2CO 4:11–4:12

Martyred believers are specially honoured in Scripture

REV 12:11 See also REV 2:13

resource.label: 9414: heaven, as community of the redeemed

title: 9023: death, of unbelievers
content:
The departure from the present life of unrepentant people to await final banishment from God.

Death is inevitable

PSA 49:10, PSA 89:48 See also 2SA 14:14; JOB 30:23; PSA 37:36; ECC 3:19–3:20, ECC 8:8

The premature death of unbelievers is the consequence of sin

PRO 10:27 OT writers, for whom God's blessing on the righteous occurs mostly in the present life, often saw early or sudden death as divine judgment on the wicked. See also 1CH 10:13; JOB 22:16; PSA 9:17, PSA 37:1–37:2, PSA 37:22, PSA 37:38, PSA 55:23, PSA 139:19; PRO 5:21–5:23, PRO 11:19, PRO 14:12; ISA 17:14; EZK 3:19, EZK 18:4, EZK 18:20; DAN 5:30; LUK 12:20

Aspects of the death of unbelievers

They die unforgiven

JHN 8:21, JHN 8:24

They die without hope

PRO 11:7; ; ECC 8:10

God has no pleasure in their death

EZK 18:23 See also EZK 18:32; 2PE 3:9

Descriptions of the death of unbelievers

Being destroyed

MAT 7:13; ; ROM 9:22

Perishing

JHN 3:16; ; 2PE 2:12

Being in fire

LUK 16:22–16:24; ; HEB 10:27

Being in darkness

MAT 25:30; ; 2PE 2:17

The consequences of the death of unbelievers

The intermediate state of awaiting judgment

REV 20:13 See also LUK 16:23; JHN 5:29; ACT 24:15

The judgment

HEB 9:27 See also JHN 5:29

The eternal consequences

DAN 12:2; MAT 25:41 See also MAT 18:8, MAT 25:46; MRK 9:45–9:48; GAL 1:8–1:9; HEB 6:2; JUD 1:7

The challenge of the death of unbelievers

The call to prepare

2KI 20:1 See also ECC 7:17, ECC 9:10; AMO 4:12

The only escape is through Jesus Christ

JHN 8:51 Death for believers is swallowed up in life, so that they are never personally aware of the pain and sting of death (compare Jn 11:26). See also JHN 5:24

title: 9024: death, spiritual
content:
A condition in this life of alienation from God because of sin. It leads ultimately to the “second death”, the permanent separation of unbelievers from God.

Spiritual death means alienation from God in this life

EPH 2:1–2:3; COL 1:21 See also MAT 8:22; ROM 6:23; COL 2:13

Spiritual death is a result of Adam's sin

ROM 5:15–5:16 See also ROM 5:12

Symptoms of spiritual death

Spiritual ignorance

MAT 4:16; ; ISA 9:2; ; LUK 1:79; ; EPH 5:8

A mind controlled by the sinful nature

ROM 8:5–8:8 See also PRO 8:36, PRO 14:12, PRO 16:25, PRO 21:16; EPH 4:18

Unbelief

JHN 3:36; ; 1JN 5:12

Living in sinful pleasures

LUK 15:13; ; 1TI 5:6

Acting in ways that lead to death

HEB 6:1, HEB 9:14

Spiritual death leads ultimately to the ``second death''

REV 2:11, REV 20:6, REV 20:14, REV 21:8

A church may be spiritually dead

REV 3:1

Deliverance from spiritual death is only through Jesus Christ

EPH 2:4–2:5 See also EZK 37:1–37:14; LUK 15:24, LUK 15:32; JHN 5:24–5:25; ROM 6:3–6:4, ROM 6:13

title: 9030: dead bodies
content:
Dead bodies were to be treated with respect

2SA 21:12–21:14; ; ECC 6:3

Unburied dead bodies were a sign of God's curse upon Israel

DEU 28:26; ; JER 16:4

Contact with dead bodies

The high priest was not to touch a dead body

LEV 21:11

Contact with a dead body made a person unclean

NUM 19:11 See also NUM 9:6–9:10, NUM 19:16

Jesus Christ was willing to touch a dead body

MAT 9:25 parallels MRK 5:41, LUK 8:54; ; MRK 9:26–9:27; ; LUK 7:14

The reluctance to approach dead bodies because of the smell

JHN 11:39

title: 9040: grave, the
content:
An OT synonym for death, the natural, inevitable human destiny, though not without hope for the righteous.

The grave as a place of burial

NUM 19:16, NUM 19:18; ; DEU 34:6; ; 1KI 13:31; ; 2KI 21:26; ; ISA 22:16

The grave representing natural death

GEN 37:35; PSA 88:3 See also GEN 42:38, GEN 44:29; 2SA 22:5–22:6; 1KI 2:6, 1KI 2:9; JOB 3:21–3:22, JOB 5:26, JOB 10:19, JOB 17:1; PSA 30:3, PSA 89:48; PRO 5:5, PRO 9:18, PRO 15:24, PRO 30:16; ISA 28:15, ISA 28:18; JER 20:17; HAB 2:5

The grave as judgment

PSA 55:15 See also NUM 16:30, NUM 16:33; JOB 24:19, JOB 40:13; PSA 9:17, PSA 31:17, PSA 49:14; ISA 5:14, ISA 14:9, ISA 14:11, ISA 14:15

The grave represented only hopelessness and oblivion before Jesus Christ's resurrection

JOB 17:13–17:16; ECC 9:10 See also JOB 7:9; PSA 6:5, PSA 31:17, PSA 88:5, PSA 88:11; ISA 38:18

The OT hope of redemption from the grave anticipated the gospel message

PSA 49:15; 1CO 15:55 See also 1SA 2:6; JOB 14:13; HOS 13:14; ACT 2:27; PSA 16:10

title: 9050: tombs
content:
Graves or burial places, often cut out of rock. People were usually buried in family tombs and kings were often buried in royal tombs.

Tombs cut out of rock

ISA 22:15–22:16 See also 2KI 23:16; 2CH 16:14; MAT 27:59–27:60 parallels MRK 15:46, LUK 23:53

Caves used as tombs

GEN 50:13 See also GEN 23:17–23:20, GEN 25:8–25:10; JHN 11:38

Tombs sealed with a stone

JHN 11:38 See also MAT 27:60 parallel MRK 15:46; MAT 27:64–27:66, MAT 28:2; MRK 16:3; LUK 24:2

Burial in a family tomb

GEN 23:19, GEN 25:8–25:10, GEN 47:29–47:30, GEN 49:29–49:32; ; JDG 8:32, JDG 16:31; ; 2SA 2:32, 2SA 17:23, 2SA 19:37, 2SA 21:12–21:14; ; 1KI 13:22

Royal tombs

2CH 16:14 See also 2CH 21:20, 2CH 24:25, 2CH 26:23, 2CH 28:27, 2CH 32:33; NEH 3:16

Tombs in gardens

2KI 21:18 See also 2KI 21:26; JHN 19:41

Contact with a tomb rendered a person ritually unclean

NUM 19:16 See also 2KI 23:6

Figurative use of tombs

MAT 23:27–23:28 See also LUK 11:43–11:44

title: 9105: last things
content:
The doctrine of the last things (“eschatology”) includes the subjects of death, the second coming of Jesus Christ, the resurrection of the dead, the last judgment, heaven and hell.

Death

Physical death is universal

JOB 30:23 See also 2SA 14:14; ROM 5:12

The timing of natural death is beyond human control

ECC 8:8 See also PSA 90:10; MAT 6:27; JAS 4:14

Death is not to be feared by the believer

PSA 23:4 See also PSA 116:15; PRO 14:32; ROM 14:8; PHP 1:21; REV 14:13

For believers, death is likened to falling asleep

JHN 11:11–11:13 See also MRK 5:39; ACT 13:36; 1CO 15:6

At the second coming, believers still living on earth will not experience death

1TH 4:15–4:17 See also 1CO 15:51–15:52

Death is the penalty for unforgiven sin

ROM 6:23 See also 1CH 10:13; PRO 11:19; ROM 5:12

The second coming of Jesus Christ

The second coming is foretold

MAT 26:64 parallel MRK 14:62 See also LUK 21:27; ACT 1:11; HEB 9:28

The timing of the second coming is known only to God the Father

MAT 24:36 parallel MRK 13:32 See also MAL 3:1; MAT 24:44 parallel LUK 12:40; REV 16:15

God's purpose at the second coming is to gather his people together, to reward the faithful and judge the wicked

MAT 16:27 See also DAN 7:13–7:14; MAT 25:31–25:32; JHN 14:3; 1CO 4:5; 1TH 4:16–4:17; 1PE 5:4; 1JN 3:2; JUD 1:14–1:15

The right attitude towards the second coming

1JN 2:28 See also MRK 13:35; ACT 3:19–3:20; 1TI 6:13–6:14; 2PE 3:11

The resurrection of the dead

All will be raised

JHN 5:28–5:29 See also DAN 12:2; ACT 24:15

Believers in Jesus Christ will be raised to eternal life

JHN 6:40 See also JHN 11:25; 2CO 4:14; 1TH 4:16

Unbelievers will be condemned

JHN 5:29 See also MAT 25:46

Believers will be given resurrection bodies

1CO 15:42–15:44 See also 1CO 15:50–15:53

The last judgment

All face judgment after death

HEB 9:27 See also ROM 14:12; 1PE 4:5; REV 20:11–20:12

Judgment is entrusted to Jesus Christ

JHN 5:22 See also ACT 10:42, ACT 17:31; REV 1:18

Those who have not responded to Christ will be condemned

JHN 12:48 See also 2TH 1:7–1:8; 2PE 3:7; JUD 1:15; REV 20:15

Believers will not be judged for sin

JHN 5:24 See also JHN 3:18; ROM 8:1–8:2, ROM 8:33–8:34

Believers will be judged on how they have lived their Christian lives

2CO 5:10 See also 1CO 4:5; HEB 9:28; 1JN 4:17

Heaven

God's throne is in heaven where he is continuously worshipped

REV 4:9–4:10 See also ISA 6:1–6:3

God's will is perfectly served in heaven

MAT 6:10 parallel LUK 11:2 See also 1KI 22:19

At Jesus Christ's second coming, a new heaven and earth will replace the old

REV 21:1

The redeemed will enjoy life in the presence of God in the new heaven

1TH 4:17 See also MAT 5:8; PHP 3:20; JUD 1:24; REV 21:1–21:4

All life in heaven is sustained by God

REV 22:1–22:2 See also JHN 6:58; REV 2:7, REV 21:23

Hell

Hell is the destiny of human beings who reject God

MAT 25:41 Hell was originally meant for the punishment of the devil and his angels. See also MAT 13:41–13:42; ROM 2:8; HEB 10:26–10:27; JUD 1:6; REV 17:8

Those in hell are finally separated from God

2TH 1:9 See also MAT 25:46

Hell is a place of fire, darkness and weeping

REV 20:15 See also MAT 8:12; 2PE 3:7; JUD 1:7

Jesus Christ himself frequently warned about the dangers of hell

LUK 12:5 See also MAT 5:29–5:30 Jesus Christ is using Eastern hyperbole, not intended literally, to stress importance; MAT 13:40

title: 9110: after-life
content:
Although the OT generally portrays this as a vague and shadowy existence, often associated with judgment, there are hints of the idea of resurrection to a fuller life which are developed in the NT. The NT points to judgment beyond death and to the believers' hope in resurrection, to a more complete enjoyment of God's presence and his blessings and to the promise of a new heaven and earth.

The after-life in the OT

The grave as a place of silence

PSA 115:17 See also PSA 6:5, PSA 30:9, PSA 88:10–88:11; ISA 38:18

The grave as a place of darkness

JOB 10:21–10:22 See also 1SA 2:9; PSA 88:12

The grave as a place of no return

JOB 7:8–7:10 See also JOB 16:22; PSA 146:4; PRO 2:18–2:19; ECC 12:7

The grave as a place of judgment

PSA 88:5 See also PSA 31:17; PRO 9:18; ISA 14:9–14:11, ISA 26:14; EZK 31:14, EZK 32:17–32:32

The grave offers peace to the righteous

JOB 3:13; ; ECC 4:2; ; ISA 57:1–57:2

The hope of resurrection

DAN 12:2 See also JOB 19:26; PSA 16:10–16:11, PSA 49:15, PSA 73:24; ISA 25:7–25:8, ISA 26:19

The after-life and God's judgment

Judgment beyond death is certain

HEB 9:27 See also 2CO 5:10; REV 20:12–20:13

There will be salvation for believers

MAT 25:19–25:23; ; HEB 9:28; ; REV 11:18

There will be separation from God for unbelievers

REV 20:15, REV 21:27 See also MAT 8:12, MAT 13:42, MAT 22:13, MAT 24:51, MAT 25:30; LUK 12:46; REV 22:15

The believing dead are already with Jesus Christ

LUK 23:43; 1TH 4:14–4:17 Believers, with Jesus Christ in spirit, await their final resurrection which will take place when he returns. See also JHN 12:26, JHN 14:3; 2CO 5:8; PHP 1:23; HEB 12:23; REV 6:9–6:11

The after-life and believers' hope of resurrection

The bodily resurrection of Jesus Christ

LUK 24:39–24:43 See also JHN 20:20, JHN 20:26–20:27; ACT 1:3; 1JN 1:1

Believers share in Christ's resurrection

ROM 8:11 See also 1CO 6:14, 1CO 15:20–15:23; 2CO 4:14

The resurrection body of believers

PHP 3:20–3:21 See also 1CO 15:23, 1CO 15:42–15:55; 2CO 5:1–5:4

The future consummation of the believers' enjoyment of God

Future glory

ROM 8:18–8:19 See also 2CO 4:17–4:18; 1JN 3:2

A new heaven and earth

2PE 3:13; ; REV 21:1

God's presence with his people

REV 21:2–21:7 The final consummation will bring God's promises to and purposes for his people to fulfilment; REV 22:14 See also EXO 25:8; REV 22:1–22:5

The Holy Spirit as a guarantee

2CO 1:22, 2CO 5:5; ; EPH 1:13–1:14

title: 9115: antichrist, the
content:
One who is directly and implacably opposed to Jesus Christ.

The antichrist will deny fundamental truths about Jesus Christ

2JN 1:7 See also MAT 24:24 parallel MRK 13:22; MRK 3:29; 1JN 2:22, 1JN 4:3

Th antichrist as a self-appointed rival to God

2TH 2:3–2:4 See also DAN 7:7–7:8, DAN 7:24–7:25, DAN 8:9–8:12, DAN 8:23–8:25, DAN 11:36–11:45; REV 13:1–13:10, REV 13:11–13:18

The coming of the antichrist is a sign of the ``last days''

1JN 2:18 See also 2TH 2:6–2:10

The final defeat of the antichrist is assured

2TH 2:8 See also DAN 7:26–7:27, DAN 8:25, DAN 11:45; REV 17:7–17:14

resource.label: 8787: opposition, to God's person and work

title: 9120: eternity
content: In Scripture eternity is not an abstraction but limitless time, over which God is totally sovereign.

title: 9121: eternity, nature of
content:
God alone possesses lasting time, in contrast to the fleeting time of the human race.

Eternity from God's perspective

PSA 90:4 See also 2PE 3:8

God's eternity is absolute

PSA 90:2 See also GEN 1:1; JHN 1:1–1:2; ROM 16:26

The name of God and eternity

EXO 3:14–3:15 See also GEN 21:33; ISA 26:4, ISA 40:28, ISA 41:4; REV 1:8

God's eternal attributes

His love

JER 31:3 See also 1JN 4:8, 1JN 4:16

His righteousness

ISA 51:6; ; 2CO 9:9

His wisdom

1CO 2:7

His power

ROM 1:20

Worship of God as eternal

JUD 1:25 See also 1CH 16:36; ROM 11:36; 2PE 3:18

Jesus Christ's eternal existence

HEB 13:8 See also JHN 17:5, JHN 17:24; HEB 1:10–1:12; PSA 102:25–102:27; HEB 5:6; PSA 110:4; HEB 7:24–7:25; REV 1:17–1:18, REV 22:13

The Holy Spirit's eternal existence

HEB 9:14

Eternity and creation

GEN 1:1 See also PRO 8:22–8:29 Wisdom (personified) is speaking here.

Eternity and the human race

PSA 39:4–39:5 See also JOB 10:5, JOB 36:26; ECC 3:11

title: 9122: eternity, and God
content:
Whether one considers the past or the future, God alone is discovered to be permanent and lasting.

God's being, his wisdom and his word are eternal

God has always existed

GEN 1:1 See also JOB 38:4; PSA 90:2; HAB 1:12; JHN 1:1–1:2

The eternity of God's wisdom

PRO 8:22–8:23 See also HEB 9:14; 1PE 1:20

God's word has always expressed his nature

PSA 119:89 See also PSA 119:160; MAT 24:35; 1PE 1:23–1:25; ISA 40:8

God's purpose to bless is eternal

2TI 1:9–1:10 See also EPH 1:3–1:11; TIT 1:2–1:3; 1PE 1:18–1:21; REV 13:8

Some aspects of the created order are eternal

MIC 6:2 See also GEN 49:26; DEU 33:15; HAB 3:6

Eternal life

God bestows eternal life

1JN 5:11 See also JHN 3:16, JHN 17:3

Resurrection of the body

1CO 15:42–15:44 See also 1CO 15:50–15:56; 2CO 5:1–5:5; 1PE 1:23

God's eternal blessings

His eternal salvation

ISA 45:17; ; HEB 5:9

His eternal covenant

ISA 54:10; ; JER 32:40; ; HEB 13:20

His eternal glory

2CO 4:17–4:18 See also 2TI 2:10; 1PE 5:10

An eternal crown

1CO 9:25; ; 1PE 5:4; ; REV 2:10

An eternal inheritance

HEB 9:15; ; 1PE 1:3–1:5

An eternal home

LUK 16:9; ; JHN 14:2–14:3

The eternal kingdom of God

God's eternal rule

1TI 1:17 See also PSA 10:16; JER 10:10; REV 15:3

The Messiah's eternal kingdom

ISA 9:6–9:7; ; LUK 1:32–1:33

Jesus Christ's eternal kingdom

2PE 1:11 See also REV 11:15

Eternal punishment

MAT 25:46 See also DAN 12:2; MAT 25:41; JUD 1:6–1:7, JUD 1:13; REV 14:11

title: 9125: footstool
content:
Used mostly symbolically to refer to God's supreme authority over his creation. It is used especially to refer to his final victory over all those who oppose him.

The footstool as a rest for the feet

2CH 9:18; JAS 2:3

The footstool used symbolically

God's footstool as a symbol for the ark and the temple

PSA 132:7 See also 1CH 28:2; PSA 99:5; LAM 2:1

God's footstool as a symbol for the earth

ISA 66:1 God cannot be confined to any man-made temple. See also MAT 5:35; ACT 7:49

Defeated enemies can be regarded as a footstool

JOS 10:24–10:25; ; 1KI 5:3; ; PSA 66:12; ; ISA 51:23; ; MIC 7:10

Jesus Christ will make all God's enemies his footstool

HEB 1:13 This passage affirms Jesus Christ's supremacy over the angels. See also PSA 110:1; MAT 22:44 parallels MRK 12:36, LUK 20:43; ACT 2:34–2:35; EPH 1:22

Jesus Christ will complete his victory when he returns

HEB 10:13 See also 1CO 15:25; HEB 2:8; PSA 8:6

title: 9130: future, the
content:
God's promises about the future are certain because it is under God's control. Christians should live on the basis that these promises, which focus on the return of Jesus Christ, will be fulfilled.

God controls the future

God knows the future

EXO 3:19–3:20 See also DEU 31:21; 2SA 7:19 parallel 1CH 17:17; PSA 139:4; ISA 42:9, ISA 46:10; DAN 2:28; ACT 2:23, ACT 3:18

God can reveal the future

DAN 2:45 See also GEN 15:13–15:16, GEN 41:25; 1SA 9:15–9:16; 2KI 8:10; DAN 8:19, DAN 10:14; MAT 2:13; ACT 18:9–18:10, ACT 27:22–27:24

The future is unknown to human beings

ECC 8:7 See also PRO 27:1; ECC 3:22, ECC 7:14, ECC 9:12, ECC 10:14; MAT 24:42–24:43; ACT 20:22; JAS 4:14

There is a future hope for believers

It is based on God's promise

JER 31:17 See also PSA 37:37; PRO 23:18, PRO 24:14; ISA 60:15

Future blessings are guaranteed

JER 29:11 See also GEN 17:7; LEV 26:3–26:5; DEU 6:2, DEU 30:9; ISA 30:23, ISA 65:23; AMO 9:13

Future blessings are superior to present experiences

PHP 1:21–1:23 See also GEN 22:17–22:18; MAT 19:29 parallels MRK 10:29–10:30, LUK 18:29–18:30; ROM 8:18–8:25; 1PE 5:4; 1JN 3:2

Key aspects of future hope

Believers will go to heaven

1PE 1:4 See also LUK 16:22, LUK 23:43; JHN 14:2–14:3; 2CO 5:1; COL 1:5; 1TH 4:16–4:17

Jesus Christ will come again

ACT 1:11 See also DAN 7:13; MAT 24:30 parallels MRK 13:26, LUK 21:27; PHP 3:20; 1TH 4:16; 2TH 2:1; HEB 9:28; JAS 5:8

The dead will rise

1CO 15:52 See also DAN 12:2; JHN 5:28–5:29, JHN 6:39–6:40; ACT 24:15; 2CO 4:14; REV 20:4–20:5, REV 20:12

Final judgment

2CO 5:10 See also PSA 62:12; MAT 16:27, MAT 25:31–25:32; HEB 9:27; 2PE 3:7; 1JN 4:17; JUD 1:14–1:15; REV 20:13

The devil and evil will be destroyed

ISA 25:7–25:8 See also 2TH 2:8; HEB 2:14; REV 7:16, REV 11:18, REV 19:20–19:21, REV 20:10

God's kingdom will be fully established

REV 11:15 See also ISA 11:6–11:9; ZEC 14:9; 1CO 15:24; REV 21:1–21:5

Believers' responsibility towards future generations

JOS 4:6–4:7 The twelve stones taken from the bed of the river Jordan is one of several instances where God's people are told to preserve memorials of present blessings for the sake of future generations. See also EXO 12:25–12:27, EXO 16:32–16:33; DEU 6:20–6:21; JOS 4:21–4:22, JOS 22:27; PSA 22:30, PSA 45:17

Proper attitudes towards the future

Looking forwards rather than backwards

PHP 3:13–3:14 See also GEN 19:17; ISA 43:18–43:19; COL 3:1–3:2; HEB 12:1–12:2

Having perseverance and faith

1CO 15:58 See also GAL 6:9; HEB 11:6–11:8, HEB 11:17–11:19; 1PE 1:13; REV 3:11

Avoiding occult predictions and false prophecies

JER 27:9 See also ISA 44:25–44:26; JER 29:8; EZK 13:3–13:7; ZEC 10:2; ACT 16:16–16:18

title: 9135: immortality
content: The quality of enduring into eternity, which Scripture insists belongs to God alone. However, God bestows the gift of eternal life and immortality upon those who believe and trust in him.

title: 9136: immortality, in OT
content:
The OT stresses that God alone is immortal and indicates that this gift may be granted to human beings under certain conditions.

Immortality is an attribute of God

God is everlasting in himself

PSA 90:2 See also NEH 9:5; PSA 41:13; ISA 9:6, ISA 40:28; HAB 1:12

God is everlasting in his character and actions

DEU 33:27 The way in which Scripture emphasises the character of God in these contexts shows that immortality is about a quality of being as well as about infinite duration. GEN 9:16, GEN 17:7, GEN 17:13, GEN 17:19; ; 1CH 16:17 parallel PSA 105:10; PSA 9:7, PSA 10:16, PSA 132:12; ; DAN 7:14, DAN 7:27; PSA 52:8, PSA 103:17; ; ISA 54:10; ; JER 31:3; PSA 119:142; ; DAN 9:24; ISA 45:17 God's everlasting salvation; ; ISA 54:8 God's everlasting kindness; ; ISA 60:19–60:20 the LORD as the everlasting light

Immortality is conditionally offered to human beings

GEN 3:22–3:23 See also PSA 6:5, PSA 88:5, PSA 88:10, PSA 115:17; DAN 12:2 The most specific OT reference to immortality. Note that while resurrection to judgment is for all, immortality is only for the righteous (Pr 12:28).

Enoch and Elijah: two special cases

GEN 5:24; ; 2KI 2:11

The confidence of believers

PSA 16:9–16:11 See also PSA 17:15, PSA 21:6, PSA 73:24, PSA 133:3

God will abolish death

ISA 25:8

title: 9137: immortality, in NT
content:
In the NT, immortal life is offered to human beings through Jesus Christ and guaranteed by the Holy Spirit. It is closely associated with eternal life and resurrection, but is seen more as a quality of being rather than in terms of unlimited extent.

Immortality is an attribute of God

1TI 1:17 See also ROM 1:23; 1TI 6:16

Immortality is offered to human beings through Jesus Christ

2TI 1:10 See also JHN 6:50, JHN 8:51, JHN 11:26; 1CO 15:22, 1CO 15:54

Immortality is guaranteed by the Holy Spirit

2CO 5:1–5:5

The association of immortality with eternal life

ROM 2:7

The association of immortality with resurrection

LUK 20:36 See also 1CO 15:42–15:43, 1CO 15:53–15:54; 1TH 4:17; REV 20:4–20:6

Immortality implied by Jesus Christ's transfiguration

MAT 17:3 parallels MRK 9:4, LUK 9:30–9:31 the presence of Moses and Elijah

Immortality as a quality of being

It is inseparable from God's other characteristics

ROM 1:23; ; 1TI 1:17, 1TI 6:16

It is inseparable from humanity's final state of existence

LUK 20:36; ; ROM 2:7; ; 1PE 1:4

title: 9140: last days
content:
The final epoch of history which is marked by the coming of the Messiah and the establishment of God's kingdom. In the NT it is portrayed as the period between Jesus Christ's first coming and the consummation of all things at his return, and is marked by godlessness and the persecution of God's people.

OT expectation of the last days

The Messianic kingdom

ISA 2:2–2:4 parallel MIC 4:1–4:3 See also ISA 9:6–9:7, ISA 11:1–11:9, ISA 65:20–65:25; JER 33:15

The restoration of Israel

JER 33:16; HOS 3:5 See also ISA 25:9; JER 30:9, JER 50:4; JOL 3:1; ZEC 8:23

The day of the LORD

ISA 13:6–13:12; ; EZK 30:3; ; JOL 2:11, JOL 2:30–2:31; ; AMO 5:18–5:20; ; ACT 2:19–2:20

Jesus Christ's coming introduces the last days

The timing of his revelation

1PE 1:20 See also GAL 4:4–4:5

The announcement of God's kingdom

MRK 1:15 See also MAT 12:28 parallel LUK 11:20; LUK 4:43, LUK 8:1

The fulfilment of Messianic promise

LUK 4:18–4:21 See also ISA 61:1–61:2; MAT 11:4–11:5 parallel LUK 7:22

The outpouring of the Spirit

ACT 2:16–2:18 See also JOL 2:28–2:29; EZK 36:27

Revelation in the last days

HEB 1:1–1:2 See also MAT 13:16–13:17 parallel LUK 10:23–10:24; ROM 16:25–16:26; EPH 3:9; COL 1:26

Redemption in the last days

HEB 9:26 See also DAN 9:24; ACT 2:21; JOL 2:32; 2CO 6:2; ISA 49:8; 1PE 1:18–1:20

Features of the last days

Godlessness

2TI 3:1–3:5 See also DAN 12:10

Deception and apostasy

1TI 4:1 See also MAT 24:4–24:5 parallels MRK 13:5, LUK 21:8; MAT 24:10–24:12; 1JN 2:18–2:19

Scoffing

2PE 3:3–3:4; ; JUD 1:18–1:19

Persecution

DAN 11:33–11:35; ; MRK 10:30; ; LUK 21:12–21:17 parallels MAT 24:9–24:10, MRK 13:9–13:13

Jesus Christ's return brings the last days to a close

It ushers in a new era

HEB 9:28 See also MAT 13:49, MAT 24:3

It is identified with the day of the LORD

2CO 1:14; ; 1TH 5:2; ; 2PE 3:10

Living in the last days

HEB 6:5 Between Jesus Christ's comings, believers have a foretaste of the age to come. See also EPH 1:19–1:21; COL 3:1–3:4; HEB 10:24–10:25

title: 9145: Messianic age
content:
In the OT, writers look forward to the era that will be inaugurated by God's decisive intervention in human history, in order to establish his eternal kingdom under the Messiah. The Messianic age is characterised by righteousness, justice and peace, by the outpouring of the Holy Spirit and by the restoration and renewal of God's people and of creation. In the NT, the idea appears in a developed form as the “kingdom of God”, inaugurated by the first coming of Jesus Christ and to be consummated at his return.

The victory of God's kingdom foreseen

DAN 2:37–2:44 See also ZEC 14:9

The victory of God's Messiah

PSA 45:3–45:6 This psalm came to be applied to the Messiah. See also PSA 110:5–110:6 This psalm was also applied to the Messiah in the OT times; ISA 9:3–9:5, ISA 42:13, ISA 59:17–59:19

The Messiah's universal reign

DAN 7:13–7:14 PSA 2:8–2:9, PSA 72:8–72:11

Restoration in the Messianic age

Restoration of harmony in creation

ISA 11:6–11:9 parallel ISA 65:25

Restoration of the land's prosperity

AMO 9:13–9:15 See also PSA 72:3; ISA 4:2; JOL 3:18

Restoration and renewal of God's people

JER 3:18; MIC 4:6–4:8 See also ISA 60:1–60:3; JOL 3:16–3:17; AMO 9:11; OBA 1:17–1:21; ACT 1:6; ZEC 3:9HOS 3:5; JER 50:4–50:5; EZK 11:19–11:20 parallel EZK 36:28

Characteristics of the Messianic age

Righteousness and justice

ISA 32:1 See also PSA 72:2, PSA 72:12–72:13; ISA 9:7, ISA 11:3–11:5, ISA 32:16; JER 23:5–23:6 parallel JER 33:15–33:16

Peace and security

ISA 32:17–32:18 See also MIC 4:3–4:4 parallel ISA 2:4; ISA 65:20–65:23; JER 23:5–23:6, JER 33:15–33:16; EZK 34:25–34:29; ZEC 9:9–9:10

Universal acknowledgment of the LORD

JER 3:17 See also ISA 2:2–2:3 parallel MIC 4:1–4:2; ISA 19:23–19:25; ZEC 14:16

The outpouring of the Holy Spirit

JOL 2:28–2:29 See also ISA 11:2, ISA 32:15, ISA 44:3, ISA 59:21; EZK 36:27

The kingdom of God in the NT

Inaugurated at Jesus Christ's first coming

MAT 12:28 parallel LUK 11:20 See also MRK 1:15; LUK 4:43

Includes the restoration of all things

ACT 3:21 See also REV 21:1–21:4

Consummated at Jesus Christ's return

REV 11:15 See also MAT 24:30–24:31 parallel MRK 13:26–13:27; REV 12:10

title: 9150: Messianic banquet
content:
A symbolic portrayal of the blessings of the age to come in which those chosen by God share in a rich feast with the Messiah. In the NT this is often pictured as a marriage supper with Jesus Christ as the groom and the church as both bride and invited guests. The feast, which will take place after the consummation of God's kingdom, is prefigured in the Lord's Supper.

The Messianic banquet promised

ISA 25:6; LUK 14:15

The significance of banqueting

As a sign of spiritual blessing

PSA 23:5 See also JOB 36:16; ISA 1:19, ISA 55:1–55:2; JOL 2:24–2:26

As a sign of spiritual satisfaction

PSA 63:5 See also PSA 107:9; ISA 58:11; EZK 34:14; JHN 7:37–7:38; REV 7:16–7:17

As a sign of fellowship with God

REV 3:20 See also EXO 24:11 This may have been a covenant meal to seal the covenant between God and Israel; SNG 2:4

The Messianic banquet as a wedding feast

The invitation to participate

MAT 22:2; REV 19:7–19:9 See also MAT 25:1–25:10; LUK 14:16; REV 21:2, REV 21:9–21:10

The invitation rejected

MAT 22:3–22:6; ; LUK 14:16–14:20

The invitation given to others

MAT 8:11–8:12 See also MAT 22:7–22:14; LUK 13:28–13:29, LUK 14:21–14:24

The anticipation of the Messianic banquet

Celebrating God's goodness

DEU 16:15 See also NEH 8:10–8:12; ZEC 14:16

It is prefigured in the Lord's Supper

LUK 22:15–22:16 The Messianic banquet is here associated with the Passover meal which Jesus Christ shared with his disciples; MAT 26:27–26:29 parallels MRK 14:23–14:25, LUK 22:17–22:18JHN 6:48–6:51 See also LUK 22:29–22:30; JHN 6:55–6:56; 1CO 11:26

title: 9155: millennium
content:
The period of 1,000 years referred to in Revelation chapter 20, during which Satan is bound and believers reign with Jesus Christ. This millennium is understood in different ways: as a period of unparalleled peace and prosperity for the church and the gospel at the end of this age, after which Christ will return (postmillennialism); as the time following Christ's return when he will reign on earth (premillennialism); as a symbolic reference to the period between Christ's first and second comings (amillennialism).

Characteristics of the millennium

Satan is bound

REV 20:1–20:3 “the Abyss” is the subterranean place of confinement for Satan and evil spirits. See also LUK 8:31 parallels MAT 8:29, MRK 5:7

Martyred believers are raised to reign with Jesus Christ

REV 20:4–20:6 Those who have suffered for the gospel receive their promised reward. See also ROM 8:17; 2TI 2:11–2:12; REV 2:10

Satan's release and final overthrow

REV 20:7–20:10 See also EZK 38:1–38:4, EZK 38:15–38:16, EZK 38:18–38:22, EZK 39:2–39:5; ZEC 14:2; ROM 16:20

The millennium will be a golden age on earth

ISA 2:2–2:4 parallel MIC 4:1–4:3 The OT looks forward to a period of universal peace and prosperity on earth. Both pre- and postmillennialists see this as a reference to Jesus Christ's future earthly reign. See also ISA 11:6–11:9, ISA 51:4–51:5, ISA 65:25

The premillennial interpretation

The premillennial order of events

REV 19:11–19:13 Jesus Christ's return. Premillennialists understand the events of Revelation chapter 19 to precede those of Revelation chapter 20; ; REV 20:2–20:3 Satan bound for 1,000 years; ; REV 20:4–20:6 a “first resurrection” of believers to share Jesus Christ's reign over the earth; ; REV 20:7–20:10 Satan's release and final overthrow; ; REV 20:11–20:15 the general resurrection of all people to face God's judgment

A first resurrection of believers

1CO 15:22–15:25 This passage allows the idea of a gap between the resurrection of believers and the eternal age. See also 1TH 4:16–4:17

God's future earthly reign

ZEC 14:9 See also DAN 2:44; ZEP 3:11; ZEC 14:16–14:21

Believers will reign with Jesus Christ

MAT 19:28 Premillennialists take this to refer to an earthly reign. See also LUK 22:29–22:30; 1CO 6:2; REV 5:9–5:10

Difficulties with premillennialism

Scriptures that depict a single resurrection of both the righteous and the wicked: DAN 12:2; ; JHN 5:28–5:29; MAT 13:40–13:43, MAT 13:49–13:50, MAT 25:31–25:32

The postmillennial interpretation

The rapid spread of the gospel

MAT 24:14 parallel MRK 13:10 See also MAT 13:31–13:33 parallels MRK 4:31–4:32, LUK 13:18–13:21

A future turning to God

PSA 22:27–22:28; ; ISA 19:19–19:24; ; ZEC 2:11

Difficulties with postmillennialism

The fortunes of the gospel and the church appear to be at a low ebb before Jesus Christ returns: MAT 24:9–24:12 parallel LUK 21:12–21:17; ; 2TI 3:1–3:5, 2TI 3:12–3:13; MAT 28:18; ; ACT 2:33–2:36, ACT 7:55–7:56

The amillennial interpretation

Satan was defeated at Jesus Christ's first coming

JHN 12:31 See also MAT 12:29 parallels MRK 3:27, LUK 11:21–11:22; LUK 10:18–10:20; COL 2:14–2:15

Interpreting the ``first resurrection''

As a reference to the soul entering heaven after death: LUK 23:43; ; 2TI 4:7–4:8; ROM 6:5; ; EPH 2:6; ; COL 3:1

Dangers of the amillennialist view

The future hope of believers is not only a spiritual reality: ROM 8:19–8:21; ; REV 21:1; 1PE 5:8; ; 1JN 5:19; ; REV 12:17

title: 9160: new heavens and new earth
content:
A way of referring to the new creation that will come about at the end of time. The theme of the restoration and re-creation of the world is a central aspect of the Christian hope.

The new heavens and new earth will replace the old

ISA 65:17 See also HEB 12:26–12:27; 2PE 3:12–3:13; REV 21:1

The nature of the new heavens and new earth

Their permanence

ISA 66:22 See also HEB 12:28

The place of God's presence

REV 21:3

The effects of the fall will be reversed

REV 21:4 See also ISA 65:20, ISA 65:23, ISA 65:25

title: 9165: restoration
content:
The returning of something or someone to their original state. Through the redeeming work of Jesus Christ, both humanity and creation will eventually be restored.

Restoration of human beings through the ministry of Jesus Christ

Restoration of health

LUK 4:38–4:39, LUK 8:43–8:44, LUK 14:2–14:4

Restoration of life

LUK 7:11–7:15; ; JHN 11:1–11:44

Restoration of sight

MRK 8:22–8:25; ; JHN 9:1–9:7

Restoration of hearing

MRK 7:32–7:35

The restoration of creation

ACT 3:21 See also ISA 11:6–11:9, ISA 41:17–41:20, ISA 55:10–55:13; REV 21:1–21:4, REV 22:1–22:5

The restoration of Israel

PSA 14:7 See also PSA 69:35–69:36; ISA 41:13, ISA 51:1–51:6; JER 30:18–30:22; EZK 36:8–36:12; AMO 9:13–9:15; ROM 11:25–11:27

Restoration in everyday life

The restoration of status

GEN 40:13; ; JOB 8:6

The restoration of fortunes

DEU 30:3; ; EZK 16:53

The restoration of buildings

2CH 24:4; ; EZR 5:9

The restoration of authority

2SA 8:3

resource.label: 7135: Israel, as the people of God

title: 9170: signs of the times
content:
Portents marking significant historical events. In Scripture the phrase refers principally to signs indicating the coming of the Messiah and the kingdom of God (both in the ministry of Jesus Christ and at the end of time).

Signs of the times signifying the future deliverance of Judah

2KI 19:29–19:31 parallel ISA 37:30–37:32 The fruitfulness of the earth is to be a sign to Hezekiah that there will be a fruitful remnant of Judah.

Signs of the times in the life of Jesus Christ

The virgin birth of Jesus Christ

ISA 7:14 See also MAT 1:22–1:23

The miracles of Jesus Christ

MAT 12:28 parallel LUK 11:20 See also JHN 2:11, JHN 2:23, JHN 3:2, JHN 4:54, JHN 6:2, JHN 6:14, JHN 9:16, JHN 11:47, JHN 12:17–12:18, JHN 20:30

Jesus Christ's resurrection

MAT 12:38–12:40 parallel LUK 11:29–11:30 See also MAT 16:4; JHN 2:18–2:22

The Jews failed to perceive the signs of the times accompanying Jesus Christ's coming

MAT 16:1–16:4 parallel MRK 8:11–8:13 See also LUK 12:54–12:56; JHN 12:37

Signs of the times at the end of the age

MAT 24:3–24:14 parallels MRK 13:3–13:13, LUK 21:7–21:19LUK 21:25–21:28 parallels MAT 24:29–24:30, MRK 13:24–13:26

It is impossible to predict the date of Jesus Christ's return from the signs of the times

1TH 5:1–5:2

Signs of the times signifying judgment

See also ISA 20:3–20:4; MAT 24:15–24:25 parallels MRK 13:14–13:23, LUK 21:20–21:24 The fall of Jerusalem is itself a sign of the judgment of all people at the end of time.

Interpreting the signs of the times

MRK 13:28–13:29 parallels MAT 24:32–24:33, LUK 21:29–21:31 See also 1CH 12:32

title: 9200: Judgment
content:
• 9210: judgment, God's
  • 9220: day of the LORD
  • 9230: judgment seat
  • 9240: last judgment
  • 9250: woe

title: 9210: judgment, God's
content:
God judges the world by identifying and condemning sin and by vindicating and rewarding the righteous. God exercises temporal judgment on the world and on his people; final judgment will take place when Jesus Christ returns.

The nature of God's judgment

Its certainty

ECC 12:14 See also PSA 7:11; ECC 3:17, ECC 11:9; JAS 5:9

Its righteousness

GEN 18:25 See also PSA 9:7–9:8, PSA 50:6, PSA 96:13 parallel 1CH 16:33

Its impartiality

2CH 19:7 See also DEU 10:17; JOB 34:19; PSA 98:9; ROM 2:11; EPH 6:9

The purpose of God's judgment

To display his glory

ISA 5:16 See also EXO 14:4; ISA 59:18–59:19; EZK 7:27, EZK 38:23; REV 14:7

To vindicate the righteous

1SA 24:12–24:15 See also PSA 7:8–7:9; ISA 34:8; JER 11:20, JER 51:9–51:10; REV 16:5–16:7

To defend the weak

PSA 140:12 See also PSA 82:1–82:4; ISA 11:4; EZK 34:16–34:22

To bring salvation to his people

ISA 30:18 See also EXO 6:6; DEU 32:36; PSA 76:8–76:9, PSA 105:5–105:7 parallel 1CH 16:12–16:14; ISA 33:22

To punish sin

ROM 2:12 See also PSA 1:4–1:6; JHN 12:48; ROM 5:16; HEB 10:26–10:30, HEB 13:4

To turn people to God

ISA 19:22 Temporal judgments may turn people to God and so save them from eternal judgment. See also 1KI 8:33 parallel 2CH 6:24; 2CH 7:13–7:14; DAN 4:33–4:34; HOS 2:5–2:7; 1CO 11:29–11:32

God's judgment may be delayed

JOB 24:1–24:4; 2PE 3:9 See also PSA 74:10–74:11, PSA 94:2–94:3; HAB 1:2–1:4, HAB 1:13; ACT 17:30; ROM 3:25; REV 6:10

Examples of God's judgment

Judgment on ungodly individuals

ACT 12:23 See also 2CH 21:18–21:19 Jehoram; DAN 5:26–5:28 Belshazzar; REV 2:22 Jezebel, the false prophetess

Judgment following particular sins

GEN 4:10–4:14 Cain's murder of Abel; ; NUM 20:12 Moses' and Aaron's disobedience; ; LEV 10:1–10:2 Nadab's and Abihu's disobedience; ; 1SA 25:38–25:39 Nabal's contemptuous treatment of David; ; 2SA 6:6–6:8 Uzzah's handling of the ark; ; 2KI 2:23–2:24 youths jeering at Elisha; ; 2KI 5:27 Gehazi's greed; ; 2CH 26:19–26:20 Uzziah's pride and unfaithfulness; ; ACT 5:1–5:10 Ananias' and Sapphira's lies

Judgment on peoples and nations

JOL 3:12–3:13 See also GEN 6:17, GEN 19:24–19:25EXO 7:4–7:5; NUM 33:4 1SA 2:10, 1SA 5:6ISA 10:12, ISA 37:36 parallel 2KI 19:35 ISA 34:5JER 25:12, JER 51:56 JER 25:31–25:33

God's judgment on his enemies

DEU 32:41–32:43 See also PSA 45:3–45:6; ISA 1:24; EZK 38:21–38:22; JHN 12:31; REV 20:7–20:10

God's judgment on Israel

God's warning of judgment

DEU 28:15–28:24; JER 1:16 See also LEV 26:15–26:20, LEV 26:33; JER 11:7–11:8

Plagues

NUM 16:46–16:47, NUM 21:5–21:9; ; 2SA 24:15 parallel 1CH 21:14

Drought and famine

1KI 17:1; ; HAG 1:10–1:11

Defeat by enemies

JOS 7:1–7:7; ; JDG 2:11–2:16 The cycle of sin, defeat and deliverance is repeated several times in Judges; ; 2CH 28:4–28:5

Exile

2KI 17:23; ; JER 11:17; ; LAM 1:5; ; EZK 39:23–39:24

ACT 17:31 See also DAN 12:2; MAT 25:31–25:32; JHN 12:48; ROM 2:16, ROM 14:10; 1CO 4:5; 2TI 4:1; 1PE 4:5; REV 20:11–20:15

title: 9220: day of the LORD
content:
The occasion of God's final intervention in human affairs to punish sin, restore the faithful of his people and establish his rule over the nations. It is linked with the Messianic hope and will be fulfilled at Jesus Christ's return. This future consummation is anticipated in historical acts of judgment and, although its time is unknown, it will be heralded and accompanied by signs and by great upheavals in nature.

The day of the LORD as a day of judgment

Of universal judgment

ISA 24:21–24:22 See also ZEP 1:14–1:18

Of judgment on the nations

title: 9230: judgment seat
content:
The platform or throne from which kings and officials administer justice. God presently exercises judgment from his heavenly throne, symbolised in the OT by the ark of the covenant, and will one day bring all peoples before his judgment seat to give account of their lives and to receive their due reward.

Examples of earthly seats of judgment

PRO 20:8; JHN 19:13 parallel MAT 27:19 See also EXO 18:13–18:14; 1KI 7:7; PSA 122:5; PRO 31:23; ACT 12:21, ACT 23:3, ACT 25:23; JAS 2:6

God's judgment seat

God judges from his throne

PSA 9:4–9:5 See also PSA 9:7–9:8, PSA 11:4–11:7, PSA 45:6, PSA 97:2

The ark as a symbol of God's throne

2SA 6:2 parallel 1CH 13:6 See also EXO 25:22; 1SA 4:4; 2KI 19:15 parallel ISA 37:16; PSA 80:1, PSA 99:1

God's future judgment

2CO 5:10 God's future judgment is exercised through Jesus Christ; REV 20:11–20:13 See also DAN 7:9–7:10; JOL 3:12; MAT 25:31–25:32; ROM 14:10

Believers will share Jesus Christ's judgment throne

MAT 19:28 See also LUK 22:30; REV 20:4

content:
God's judgment of all people at the end of the age. This has been entrusted to Jesus Christ who, at his return, will reward the righteous and punish the unrighteous. Judgment will be on the basis of deeds, the response to God's revelation and faith in Jesus Christ. Believers need not fear the last judgment, but should live godly lives in anticipation of it.

The certainty of last judgment

HEB 9:27 See also ECC 3:17; ROM 14:10–14:12; 2CO 5:10; 1PE 4:5

It was anticipated in the cross

It will take place at the end of the age

MAT 13:49 See also MAT 13:39–13:43

2TH 1:6–1:7 See also MAT 25:31–25:33; 1CO 4:5; 2PE 3:10; REV 19:11

God as judge

DAN 7:9–7:10 See also HEB 12:23

God entrusts judgment to Jesus Christ

ACT 17:31 See also JHN 5:22; ACT 10:42; ROM 2:16

The result of last judgment

Two eternal destinies

DAN 12:2 See also MAT 25:46

The ungodly will be punished

2PE 3:7 See also MAT 25:41; ROM 2:5; HEB 10:26–10:28; 2PE 2:4, 2PE 2:9; JUD 1:6–1:7, JUD 1:15

The righteous will be rewarded

2TI 4:8 See also MAT 25:34; 1CO 9:25; REV 11:18

See also MAT 10:14–10:15 parallel LUK 10:11–10:12 Those towns which reject Jesus Christ's messengers will face judgment; MAT 10:32–10:33 parallel LUK 12:8–12:9 Those who disown Jesus Christ will be disowned by him; MAT 25:35–25:40; MRK 8:38; LUK 10:16; ACT 4:12, ACT 16:30–16:31

The fairness of God's last judgment

ROM 3:4–3:6 See also PSA 51:4, PSA 98:9; ISA 16:5; ROM 3:19

content:

title: 9300: Resurrection
content: • 9310: resurrection

title: 9310: resurrection
content: The raising of Jesus Christ from the dead by God after his suffering and death on the cross. Historical evidence for his resurrection includes the empty tomb and the appearance of the risen Christ to the disciples. It has significance both in relation to the identity of Jesus Christ and the future hope of believers.

title: 9311: resurrection, of Jesus Christ
content:
The resurrection of Jesus Christ is of central importance to the NT. It affirms the divinity of Jesus Christ, marks the words and deeds of his ministry with God's seal of approval and opens the way to the future resurrection of believers.

Jesus Christ's resurrection was foreshadowed in the OT

In Abraham and Isaac: GEN 22:5; ; HEB 11:19; JON 1:17, JON 2:10; ; MAT 12:40

Jesus Christ's resurrection was predicted

In the OT

1CO 15:3–15:4 See also ISA 53:11; HOS 6:2; LUK 24:45–24:46; ACT 2:25–2:31, ACT 13:35; PSA 16:8–16:11; ACT 13:34, ACT 26:22–26:23

By Jesus Christ himself

MAT 16:21 parallels MRK 8:31, LUK 9:22 See also MAT 17:9 parallel MRK 9:9; MAT 20:18–20:19 parallels MRK 10:32–10:34, LUK 18:31–18:33; MAT 26:32 parallel MRK 14:28; JHN 2:19–2:21

Jesus Christ's resurrection was anticipated by the raising of the dead

In the OT

HEB 11:35 None of the OT or NT miracles are true resurrections. Those raised resumed life as before, their bodies unchanged. Resurrection for Jesus Christ and therefore for his people means a changed body and permanent life in glory. This is the “better resurrection”. See also 1KI 17:22; 2KI 4:35, 2KI 13:21

In the miracles of Jesus Christ

LUK 7:22 parallel MAT 11:4–11:5 See also MAT 9:23–9:25 parallel MRK 5:38–5:42; MAT 27:52–27:53; LUK 7:14–7:15; JHN 11:43–11:44

The sequence of events in Jesus Christ's resurrection

It was preceded by suffering and death

PHP 2:8–2:9 See also JHN 19:28–19:33; HEB 2:9

It took place on the first day of the week

JHN 20:1–20:2 parallels MAT 28:1–28:2, MRK 16:2–16:4, LUK 24:1–24:3

It was announced by angels

MAT 28:5–28:6 parallels MRK 16:5–16:6, LUK 24:4–24:6 See also LUK 24:23; 1TI 3:16

The disciples were reluctant to believe it

LUK 24:25–24:26 See also MRK 16:13–16:14; LUK 24:11; JHN 20:24–20:25

The disciples saw the evidence and were ultimately convinced

JHN 2:22 See also LUK 24:33–24:35; JHN 20:8, JHN 20:18, JHN 20:26–20:28; ACT 2:32, ACT 3:15, ACT 4:33

The risen Christ appeared to many people

ACT 1:3 MAT 28:16–28:17; ; MRK 16:14; ; LUK 24:50–24:52; ; JHN 20:19–20:23, JHN 20:26–20:31, JHN 21:1–21:2; ; ACT 1:9–1:11; MRK 16:9; ; JHN 20:11–20:18; LUK 24:13–24:15 to two on the Emmaus road; LUK 24:34; ; 1CO 15:5; ACT 9:3–9:5 parallels ACT 22:6–22:8, ACT 26:12–26:15; ; 1CO 15:8; ACT 10:39–10:41; ; 1CO 15:6 to over 500 people; ; 1CO 15:7 to James

The resurrection was followed by Jesus Christ's entry into glory

ROM 8:34 See also 1CO 15:24–15:29; EPH 1:18–1:21; 1TI 3:16; HEB 1:3; REV 1:18

Jesus Christ's resurrection body was spiritual but not ghostly

LUK 24:37–24:39 See also JHN 20:27; ACT 1:4; 1CO 15:50; PHP 3:21

title: 9312: resurrection, significance of Jesus Christ's
content:
Jesus Christ's resurrection represents a demonstration of the power of God, the confirmation of the divinity of Jesus Christ and the grounds of hope for Christian believers.

Jesus Christ's resurrection was a demonstration of God's power

The power of God the Father

EPH 1:18–1:20 See also MAT 22:29–22:32; ACT 2:24, ACT 3:15, ACT 10:40, ACT 13:29–13:30; GAL 1:1; COL 2:12

The power of the Holy Spirit

ROM 1:4; ; 1TI 3:16; ; 1PE 3:18

The resurrection confirmed Jesus Christ as the Son of God

JHN 20:30–20:31 John calls Jesus Christ's miracles “signs” (see also Jn 2:11; 6:2) and his resurrection is the climax, confirming his identity beyond all doubt; ROM 1:4 See also PSA 2:7; ACT 13:33

The centrality of the resurrection of Jesus Christ

As the basis of faith

1CO 15:14–15:15 See also ACT 3:15, ACT 4:33, ACT 17:18, ACT 24:21; ROM 10:9; 2TI 2:8; HEB 6:1–6:2

As the basis of believers' justification

ROM 4:25, ROM 8:34

As the basis of Christian hope

ACT 24:15; ; 1CO 15:19

As the basis of believers' resurrection

1CO 15:20–15:23 The Law of Moses (Ex 23:16) provided for an offering of the firstfruits of crops to God. The firstfruits were the guarantee of the full harvest to come. The NT sees the resurrection of Jesus Christ as the firstfruits of the full ingathering of all God's people when Jesus Christ comes again. See also JHN 14:19; ACT 26:23; ROM 8:11

content:
The gospel brings new life to men and women, who lived in death and darkness until they came to faith. The NT emphasis upon “being made alive in Christ” is closely linked to the resurrection of Jesus Christ.

JHN 5:24–5:26 See also ROM 6:3–6:5; 1CO 15:17; COL 2:13

Spiritual desires

COL 3:1–3:2

Spiritual assurance

Spiritual appetite

Commitment to God

ROM 6:13

1JN 3:14

Spiritual character

content:

In the OT

DAN 12:2

By Jesus Christ

JHN 5:28–5:29

The resurrection of the dead will occur at Jesus Christ's return

REV 20:12–20:13 See also ACT 17:31; 1CO 15:52; 1TH 4:16

Unbelief

ACT 26:8 See also MAT 22:29–22:32 parallels MRK 12:24–12:27, LUK 20:34–20:38; 1CO 15:35–15:44

title: 9315: resurrection, of believers
content:
The future event of finally being raised to glory with Jesus Christ. Believers may rest assured that, on account of their faith, they will share in the resurrection and glory of Christ and be with him for ever.

A resurrection to eternal life

JHN 11:25–11:26 See also DAN 12:3; LUK 20:35–20:36; JHN 5:24–5:25

Being completely united to Jesus Christ

ROM 6:5 See also JHN 6:39, JHN 6:44; ACT 26:23; ROM 6:8; 1CO 6:14, 1CO 15:20–15:23; 2CO 4:14; COL 3:4; 1TH 4:16; 2TI 2:11

1CO 15:49 See also PSA 17:15; MAT 22:24–22:30 parallel MRK 12:18–12:25; 1CO 15:51–15:53; PHP 3:21; 1JN 3:2

1CO 15:19; 1TH 4:13–4:14 In the NT the death of believers is often likened to sleep. See also JOB 19:23–19:27; PSA 49:15, PSA 71:20; ISA 26:19; DAN 12:3; HOS 13:14; JHN 11:24; ACT 23:6, ACT 24:15; 2CO 5:1–5:4; 2TI 1:10

1CO 15:58 See also LUK 14:12–14:14; 1CO 15:30–15:32; 2CO 5:6–5:10; PHP 1:20–1:21; HEB 11:35; 1JN 3:3

ROM 8:17; 2TI 2:11–2:12 See also JHN 12:24–12:25; ACT 14:22; 1PE 4:12–4:13

title: 9410: heaven
content: God's habitation where he is worshipped and served by angels. Solely on account of the sacrifice of Jesus Christ on the cross, believers will inherit a place in heaven and there for ever enjoy perfect fellowship with God in his worship and service.

resource.label: 9414: heaven, as community of the redeemed

title: 9411: heaven, nature of
content:
Scripture refers to heaven as God's habitation but also uses the term as an alternative for God himself.

Heaven as God's habitation

It is the place where he dwells

DEU 26:15 See also GEN 28:17; 2CH 6:21; ECC 5:2; REV 13:6

It is insufficient as God's dwelling-place

1KI 8:27 parallel 2CH 6:18 See also PSA 113:5–113:6

Heaven as the place of God's throne

PSA 11:4 See also EXO 24:9–24:11; 1KI 22:19; ISA 6:1, ISA 63:15; DAN 7:9; MAT 5:34, MAT 23:22; ACT 7:49; ISA 66:1; HEB 8:1; REV 4:1–4:6, REV 20:11

MRK 1:10 parallels MAT 3:16, LUK 3:21–3:22 See also JHN 1:32

JHN 6:38 See also JHN 3:13

LUK 24:51 parallel MRK 16:19 See also EPH 1:20

HEB 9:24 See also COL 3:1

1TH 4:16 See also MAT 25:31; ACT 1:11; 1TH 1:10

title: 9414: heaven, as community of the redeemed
content:
The community of the redeemed in heaven will represent all peoples and languages. They will owe this solely to the sacrifice of Jesus Christ. They will share in the divine life in perfect fellowship with God, free for ever from suffering and death.

Heaven as a divine gift

ROM 6:23 See also LUK 12:32, LUK 22:28–22:30; JHN 17:2

Divine preparations made for believers in heaven

JHN 14:2 See also 2CO 5:1; HEB 11:16

The redeemed in heaven come from all peoples

REV 7:9–7:10 See also ISA 59:19; MAL 1:11; MAT 8:11 parallel LUK 13:29; REV 5:9

The redeemed owe their place in heaven solely to Jesus Christ

REV 7:14 Washing one's robes in the blood of the Lamb symbolises cleansing through the sacrifice of Jesus Christ. See also 1PE 1:18–1:19; REV 22:14

The redeemed are identified with Jesus Christ

Jesus Christ acknowledges the redeemed as his own

LUK 12:8 See also MAT 7:21–7:23

Believers possess the family likeness to Jesus Christ

1CO 15:49 See also 2CO 3:18; PHP 3:21; 1JN 3:2

Believers share divine life

2PE 1:4 See also REV 2:7, REV 3:21

Believers on earth at the second coming will be taken up to heaven

REV 11:12 See also JHN 11:26; 1TH 4:16–4:17 Believers living on earth when Jesus Christ returns will not experience death, but be taken straight to heaven.

Conditions in heaven for the redeemed

Perpetual and perfect fellowship with the Lord

REV 21:3 See also PSA 17:15; MAT 5:8; JHN 14:3; 1TH 4:17; REV 22:4

Joy in the immediate presence of God

PSA 16:11 See also ISA 51:11; MAT 25:21; LUK 15:7, LUK 15:10; JHN 15:11; 1TH 2:19–2:20; HEB 12:2, HEB 12:22; JUD 1:24

Restfulness

2TH 1:5–1:7 See also HEB 4:3, HEB 4:9; REV 14:13

There will no longer be any need for the marriage relationship

REV 21:23 See also DAN 12:3; MAT 13:43; 2CO 4:17; 1PE 2:9

Divine glory will banish the memory of earthly troubles

ROM 8:18 See also 2CO 4:17; 1PE 5:1

REV 21:4 See also ISA 25:8, ISA 35:10, ISA 51:11; LUK 20:35–20:36; REV 7:17

title: 9420: book of life
content:
Book in which the names of the living were recorded and from which their names were removed at death. From the time of the exodus onwards, Scripture contains references to God's book of life. It is to contain only the names of the righteous and is to be opened on the day of judgment.

The privilege of having one's name in the book of life

LUK 10:20; HEB 12:22–12:23 See also DAN 12:1; PHP 4:3; REV 3:5, REV 21:27

The unrighteous are excluded from the book of life

EXO 32:31–32:33 See also PSA 69:27–69:28; REV 13:8, REV 17:8, REV 20:12–20:15

Other references to such a book

PSA 56:8, PSA 139:16; ; MAL 3:16

title: 9430: paradise
content:
The destination of the righteous after death

LUK 23:43 This Persian term originally described a park or pleasure garden. It was used by the Jews of the Garden of Eden, and of the future blessedness of the saints. See also 2CO 12:3–12:4; REV 2:7

title: 9500: Hell
content:

  • 9530: Hades

title: 9510: hell
content: A term used in English translations of the Bible to represent the Hebrew word “Sheol” (the place of the departed) and the Greek word “Gehenna”, which came to refer to the place of punishment for the wicked after death. The Valley of Hinnom (Hebrew “Ge Hinnom”, from which “Gehenna” is derived) on the south side of Jerusalem became a symbol of all that is hateful to God, on account of its use for human sacrifice.

resource.label: 9511: hell, as a place of punishment

title: 9511: hell, as a place of punishment
content:
The place of eternal punishment in fire and darkness intended for Satan and his angels, but also for human beings who choose to reject God.

Hell was originally created for the devil and his angels

MAT 25:41 See also LUK 8:31; 2PE 2:4; REV 20:1–20:3, REV 20:10

Hell is a consequence of rejecting God

MAT 13:40–13:42 See also DEU 32:22 Fire is often referred to in the OT as a symbol of divine judgment; MRK 9:42–9:48; JHN 3:36; ROM 2:8; 1TH 5:9; HEB 10:26–10:29; 2PE 3:7; REV 20:15, REV 21:8

Jesus Christ possesses authority over hell

MAT 16:18; REV 1:18 The Greek word “Hades” often simply means “death”. However, at points it implies the experience of punishment after death. See also JHN 5:27; ACT 10:42, ACT 17:31

The Valley of Hinnom as a figure of hell

The Valley of Hinnom as a geographical feature

JOS 15:8, JOS 18:16

The Valley of Hinnom as a place of child sacrifice

2CH 28:3 parallel 2KI 16:3 See also 2KI 23:10; 2CH 33:6 parallel 2KI 21:6; JER 32:35

The Valley of Hinnom as a place of God's judgment

JER 7:30–7:32 See also JER 19:1–19:15

The Valley of Hinnom as a synonym for hell

MAT 10:28 parallel LUK 12:4–12:5 The word used for “hell” is “Gehenna”, from the Hebrew “Ge Hinnom”, meaning “the Valley of Hinnom”. See also MAT 5:29–5:30, MAT 18:8–18:9 parallel MRK 9:43–9:48

content:
The state of final separation from God, and so from all light, love, peace, pleasure and fulfilment.

Hell is separation from God

2TH 1:8–1:10 See also MAT 7:23, MAT 8:12, MAT 25:32; REV 21:8

The final state of the wicked is one of eternal punishment

MAT 25:41–25:46 “eternal punishment” means the punishment that is part of, and belongs to, the final order of things that follows this present age. This is usually, though not invariably, understood as an unending process of punishment consciously experienced by the wicked. See also JUD 1:7

God's wrath

LUK 16:26 See also HEB 6:4–6:6, HEB 10:26–10:27; REV 16:11

content:
The reality of hell should affect the way people live.

The importance of making the right choice

MAT 7:13–7:14 See also DEU 30:19; JER 21:8; LUK 13:23–13:25

A personal spiritual life is vital

It is more important than short-term worldly gain

MAT 16:26 parallels MRK 8:36, LUK 9:25 See also LUK 12:20, LUK 16:19–16:25

It is not just outward appearance

MAT 3:7–3:10 parallel LUK 3:7–3:9

MAT 10:28 parallel LUK 12:4–12:5 See also DEU 4:23–4:24; ISA 8:12–8:13

Some attitudes and practices that lead to hell

Deliberately continuing in sin

HEB 10:26–10:27 See also NUM 15:30; HEB 6:4–6:6; 2PE 2:20

Lack of spiritual response

MAT 22:13, MAT 24:51, MAT 25:30

Wilfully ignoring divine activity

MAT 11:20–11:24 parallel LUK 10:13–10:15 Sodom, destroyed by God, was notoriously sinful. See also MAT 23:33, MAT 25:41–25:46

Contempt for fellow humans

MAT 5:22 The term “fool” implied the despising of another as worthless and godless. See also JAS 3:6

title: 9520: Abyss, the
content:
Derived from a Greek word meaning “something which is bottomless” and often used to refer to a place of despair in which demons are imprisoned.

The Abyss as a place of despair

LUK 8:31; ; REV 9:2

The Abyss as the home of demons

REV 9:11, REV 17:8

The Abyss as a prison

REV 9:1, REV 20:1, REV 20:3

title: 9530: Hades
content:
A term used in the NT to refer to the place in which departed spirits rest, while waiting for judgment.

Hades is the place where the dead wait for judgment

REV 20:13–20:14 See also MAT 11:23 parallel LUK 10:15 translated “depths”; LUK 16:23 translated “hell”ACT 2:27, ACT 2:31 REV 6:8

Jesus Christ is Lord of Hades

MAT 16:18; REV 1:18

title: 9540: Sheol
content:
A Hebrew term used to refer to the grave, the pit or the tomb. Some older translations have the term incorrectly translated as “hell”.

Sheol is the destiny of all

PSA 89:48 See also GEN 37:35, GEN 44:29; 1KI 2:9; PSA 18:5, PSA 107:20; PRO 1:12, PRO 30:16; EZK 31:15–31:17; JON 2:2

Sheol is often described in terms suggesting a grave

NUM 16:33 See also 1SA 2:6; JOB 24:19; PSA 9:17, PSA 31:17, PSA 49:14, PSA 55:15; ISA 14:15

JOB 14:13; PSA 16:10 See also PSA 30:3, PSA 49:15, PSA 86:13

title: 9600: Hope
content: • 9610: hope

title: 9610: hope
content: In Scripture, a confident expectation for the future, describing both the act of hoping and the object hoped for. When grounded in God, hope provides the motivation to live the Christian life even in the face of trouble.

title: 9611: hope, nature of
content:
Hope, in its general sense, is the anticipation of a future outcome. It is a subjective expectation which may be either firmly based or misdirected.

Hope that an event will take place

1CO 9:10; 1TI 3:14 See also EST 9:1; LUK 6:34; ACT 24:26 Felix hoped Paul would offer him a bribe; ROM 15:24 Paul hoped to visit Rome on his way to Spain; 1CO 9:15, 1CO 16:7; 2CO 1:13–1:14, 2CO 5:11, 2CO 11:1PHP 2:19, PHP 2:23 PHM 1:22; 2JN 1:12; 3JN 1:14

Hope for a positive outcome

ECC 9:4 See also RUT 1:12; 2KI 4:28; PRO 19:18; ROM 11:14

Misplaced or vain hope

PSA 33:17; JER 23:16 See also JOB 8:13–8:14, JOB 11:20; PRO 26:12, PRO 29:20; JER 50:7; 1TI 6:17

Hope removed or not satisfied

JOB 30:26; JER 8:15 See also JOB 6:19–6:20, JOB 14:7–14:12, JOB 19:10, JOB 27:8; ISA 38:18; JER 13:16, JER 14:19; LAM 3:18; EZK 37:11; ZEC 9:5; LUK 24:21 The two disciples share their hopes with Jesus Christ on the Emmaus road; 1TH 4:13

content:
A total grounding of one's confidence and expectation in God's goodness and providential care even in the face of trouble.

Hope in God is commanded

PSA 131:3; 1TI 6:17 See also PSA 31:24, PSA 130:7; ROM 12:12; HEB 10:23

Hope can be placed in Scripture as the word of God

PSA 119:74 See also PSA 119:43, PSA 119:49, PSA 119:81, PSA 119:114, PSA 119:147, PSA 130:5; ISA 42:4; ACT 26:6; ROM 15:4; COL 1:5

It brings security and confidence

resource.label: 8463: priority, of faith, hope and love

title: 9613: hope, as confidence
content:
Hope means more than a vague wish that something will happen. It is a sure and confident expectation in God's future faithfulness and presence. The horizon of Christian hope extends beyond death into an eternity prepared by God himself, the reality of which is guaranteed by Jesus Christ.

God and Jesus Christ are the hope of believers

PSA 71:5; 1TI 1:1 See also JER 14:8, JER 17:13; MAT 12:21; ISA 42:4; ACT 28:20; ROM 15:12–15:13; ISA 11:10; 1TI 4:10; 1PE 1:21

The hope of resurrection and eternal life

ACT 23:6 The Sadducees did not believe in the resurrection whereas the Pharisees did; TIT 1:2 See also ACT 2:26–2:27; PSA 16:9; ACT 24:15; ROM 8:24; 1CO 15:19; TIT 3:7; HEB 6:11, HEB 7:19; 1PE 1:3

Hope is a Christian virtue

ROM 5:3–5:4; 1CO 13:13 See also ROM 12:12, ROM 15:13; 1CO 13:7; EPH 4:4; COL 1:23; HEB 3:6; 1PE 3:15

The effect of future hope on living now

title: 9614: hope, results of its absence
content:
An absence of hope leads to a loss of vision, a sense of despondency and ultimately to despair. This contrasts sharply with the Christian hope.

Feelings produced by a lack of hope

Despair

JOB 17:13–17:15; PRO 13:12; 1CH 29:15 See also JOB 6:11, JOB 7:6; PSA 88:15–88:18; ECC 2:17; ISA 19:9, ISA 38:18; 2CO 1:8

A sense of being abandoned by God

PSA 22:1–22:2 See also LAM 3:18; EZK 37:11

A deep longing for life to end

The choice of trusting in God or futile self-effort

ROM 4:18 See also JON 1:13–1:14; JER 18:11–18:12; ACT 27:20

Suicide

MAT 27:5 See also 1SA 31:4; 2SA 17:23; 1KI 16:18
